title: Using R for the first time
text: get started R, need install R RStudio, user-friendly interface R. First, download R Comprehensive R Archive Network (CRAN), follow installation instructions operating system. installing R, download RStudio follow prompts complete installation. plenty online resources available guide step. R: https://cloud.r-project.org/ RStudio: https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/ Useful starting point RStudio Education: Finding Way R

what: Loading a Package and Basic Introduction to RStudio
title: Using R for the first time
text: first open RStudio, ’ll see layout multiple panes. main areas Console (can type execute R commands), Source Editor (write edit R scripts), Environment/History pane, Files/Plots/Packages/Help pane.

previous_headings: Loading a Package and Basic Introduction to RStudio
what: Loading a Package for the First Time
title: Using R for the first time
text: load package R first time, follow two steps: Installing package: Open Console RStudio type: Loading package: installation, load package R session using library() function:

previous_headings: Loading a Package and Basic Introduction to RStudio
title: Using R for the first time
text: Creating new R script: Click File > New File > R Script. Running code: Place cursor line code press Ctrl+Enter (Cmd+Enter Mac). Viewing installed packages: bottom-right pane, click “Packages” tab. Getting help: Type ?function_name Console. example: Viewing plots: Created plots appear “Plots” tab bottom-right pane. Managing files: Use “Files” tab bottom-right pane navigate project directory.

title: ConSciR
text: ConSciR R package specifically designed assist conservators scientists providing toolkit performing calculations streamlining common tasks conservation.

title: ConSciR
text: Calculations: dew point, absolute humidity, water vapour pressure, mixing ratio, enthalpy Graphs: TRH plots, psychometric chart Summaries: percentiles Analysis: Tidying data:

title: ConSciR
text: -- ConSciR Github page: ConSciR Github

title: ConSciR
text: ensure optimal functionality ConSciR functions, crucial maintain data tidy consistently formatted structure. allows code easily locate process variables. Please note specific naming conventions columns essential package work correctly. following formats recommended:

title: ConSciR
text: Key Points: Column Names: Try use exact column names shown (Site, Sensor, Date, Temp, RH, Lux, UV). names case-sensitive. Tools tidy files developed. Date Format: Date column ideally datetime format (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS). Check behaviour dates format hasn’t used. Numeric Data: Ensure Temp, RH, Lux, UV columns contain numeric data. Categorical Data: Site Sensor categorical variables character strings. Tidy Structure: row represents single observation, column represents single variable. Missing Columns: Include relevant columns, even data points missing (leave blank use NA missing values).

title: ConSciR
text: Add calculated values using mutate Perform calculations visualise explore data
code: # Load packages library(ConSciR) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) # Add calculated values using mutate head(mydata) |>   mutate(     AbsHum = calcAH(Temp, RH),      DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH),     MixingRatio = calcMR(Temp, RH),     Enthalpy = calcEnthalpy(Temp, RH),      IPI = calcIPI(Temp, RH),      PI = calcPI(Temp, RH)   ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH AbsHum DewPoint MixingRatio #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8   7.05     6.38        5.96 #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7   7.03     6.34        5.94 #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6   7.01     6.30        5.93 #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6   6.97     6.22        5.89 #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5   6.95     6.18        5.87 #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2   6.90     6.06        5.83 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Enthalpy <dbl>, IPI <dbl>, PI <dbl> mydata |>   # Calculate Absolute Humidity and Dew Point   mutate(     AbsHum = calcAH(Temp, RH),     DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH)   ) |>   # Create base plot using graph_TRH function   graph_TRH() +   # Add Absolute Humidity line   geom_line(aes(Date, AbsHum), color = "green") +   # Add Dew Point line   geom_line(aes(Date, DewPoint), color = "purple") +   # Set y-axis limits   lims(y = c(0, 100)) +   # Add labels and title   labs(     x = "Date",     y = "",     title = "Sensor",     subtitle = "Temperature (red), relative humidity (blue), absolute humidity (green), dew point (purple)",     caption = "ConSciR"   ) +   # Apply a theme   theme_minimal()

title: Conservation tools
text: calcMould requires dataset “Date” column.
code: TRHgrid |>   mutate(Mould_Sedlbauer = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = "Sedlbauer")) |>   filter(Mould_Sedlbauer < 100) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = Mould_Sedlbauer)) +   geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +   labs(title = "Sedlbauer model: Critical RH for mould growth from temperature") +    theme_bw() mydata |>   mutate(Mould_Sedlbauer = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = "Sedlbauer")) #> # A tibble: 35,136 × 6 #>    Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH Mould_Sedlbauer #>    <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>           <dbl> #>  1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8            73.5 #>  2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7            73.5 #>  3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6            73.5 #>  4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6            73.5 #>  5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5            73.5 #>  6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2            73.5 #>  7 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:29:59  21.7  36.3            73.5 #>  8 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:44:59  21.7  36.4            73.5 #>  9 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:59:59  21.7  36              73.5 #> 10 London Room 1 2024-01-01 02:14:59  21.6  36              73.5 #> # ℹ 35,126 more rows  mydata |>   mutate(Mould_Sedlbauer = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = "Sedlbauer")) |>   ggplot() +   geom_line(aes(Date, Mould_Sedlbauer), col = "purple") +   geom_line(aes(Date, RH), col = "blue") +    labs(title = "Sedlbauer model: Critical RH for mould growth from temperature") +    theme_bw() TRHgrid |>   mutate(Mould_VIR = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = "VIR")) |>   filter(Mould_VIR < 100) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = Mould_VIR)) +   geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +   labs(title = "VIR model: Critical RH for mould growth from temperature") +    theme_bw() mydata |>   mutate(Mould_VIR = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = "VIR")) #> # A tibble: 35,136 × 6 #>    Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH Mould_VIR #>    <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8      79.0 #>  2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7      79.0 #>  3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6      79.0 #>  4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6      79.0 #>  5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5      79.0 #>  6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2      79.0 #>  7 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:29:59  21.7  36.3      79.0 #>  8 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:44:59  21.7  36.4      79.0 #>  9 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:59:59  21.7  36        79.0 #> 10 London Room 1 2024-01-01 02:14:59  21.6  36        79.0 #> # ℹ 35,126 more rows  mydata |>   mutate(Mould_VIR = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = "VIR")) |>   ggplot() +   geom_line(aes(Date, Mould_VIR), col = "purple") +   geom_line(aes(Date, RH), col = "blue") +   labs(title = "VIR model: Critical RH for mould growth from temperature") +    theme_bw() TRHgrid |>   mutate(Mould_Daily_Dose = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = "Isaksson")) |>   filter(Mould_Daily_Dose > 0) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = Mould_Daily_Dose)) +   geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +   labs(title = "Isaksson model: Daily dose (1/t(ms)) / D(rel/day)") +    theme_bw() mydata |>   mutate(Mould_Daily_Dose = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = "Isaksson")) #> # A tibble: 35,136 × 6 #>    Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH Mould_Daily_Dose #>    <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>            <dbl> #>  1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8          -0.0140 #>  2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7          -0.0140 #>  3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6          -0.0140 #>  4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6          -0.0140 #>  5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5          -0.0140 #>  6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2          -0.0140 #>  7 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:29:59  21.7  36.3          -0.0140 #>  8 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:44:59  21.7  36.4          -0.0140 #>  9 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:59:59  21.7  36            -0.0140 #> 10 London Room 1 2024-01-01 02:14:59  21.6  36            -0.0139 #> # ℹ 35,126 more rows  mydata |>   mutate(Mould_Daily_Dose = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = "Isaksson")) |>   ggplot() +   geom_line(aes(Date, Mould_Daily_Dose), col = "purple") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col = "red") +   labs(title = "Isaksson model: Daily dose (1/t(ms)) / D(rel/day)") +    theme_bw() mydata |>   calcMould()  #> # A tibble: 35,136 × 11 #>    Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH TempMould RHMould days_mould #>    <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8        22      37         NA #>  2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7        22      37         NA #>  3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6        22      37         NA #>  4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6        22      37         NA #>  5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5        22      36         NA #>  6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2        22      36         NA #>  7 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:29:59  21.7  36.3        22      36         NA #>  8 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:44:59  21.7  36.4        22      36         NA #>  9 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:59:59  21.7  36          22      36         NA #> 10 London Room 1 2024-01-01 02:14:59  21.6  36          22      36         NA #> # ℹ 35,126 more rows #> # ℹ 3 more variables: time_diff <drtn>, mould_prob <dbl>, mould <dbl>  mydata |>   calcMould() |>   ggplot() +   geom_point(aes(Date, days_mould), col = "purple", size = 1) +    labs(title = "IPI model: Days to mould") +    theme_bw() mydata |>   calcMould() |>   ggplot() +   geom_area(aes(Date, mould), col = "purple") +    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, col = "red") +   labs(title = "IPI model: Mould probability") +    theme_bw() mydata |>   calcMould() |>   ggplot() +   geom_line(aes(Date, mould), col = "purple") +    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, col = "red") +   scale_y_log10() +    labs(title = "IPI model: Mould probability (log scale)") +    theme_bw()

title: Conservation tools
code: TRHgrid |>   mutate(LifeTime = calcLM(Temp, RH)) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = LifeTime)) +   geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +   theme_minimal() TRHgrid |>   mutate(LM_log = calcLM(Temp, RH) |> log10()) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = LM_log)) +   geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +   theme_minimal()

title: Conservation tools
code: TRHgrid |>   mutate(PI = calcPI(Temp, RH)) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = PI)) +   geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +   theme_minimal() TRHgrid |>   mutate(PI_log10 = calcPI(Temp, RH) |> log10()) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = PI_log10)) +   geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +   theme_minimal()

what: TRHgrid: Dataset used to visualise the functions
title: Humidity functions
text: grid temperature relative humidity values used analysis relationship derived variables. grid temperature humidity -50 100°C 0 100%RH 0.25°C 1%RH spacing. expand.grid() function creates complete factorial combination temperature relative humidity values, unique temperature-humidity pair represented..
code: Temp <- seq(-50, 100, 0.25) RH <- seq(0, 100, 1)  TRHgrid <-    expand.grid(Temp, RH) |>    tibble() |>    rename(Temp = Var1, RH = Var2)   summary(TRHgrid) #>       Temp             RH      #>  Min.   :-50.0   Min.   :  0   #>  1st Qu.:-12.5   1st Qu.: 25   #>  Median : 25.0   Median : 50   #>  Mean   : 25.0   Mean   : 50   #>  3rd Qu.: 62.5   3rd Qu.: 75   #>  Max.   :100.0   Max.   :100  head(TRHgrid, 3)  #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>    Temp    RH #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 -50       0 #> 2 -49.8     0 #> 3 -49.5     0 tail(TRHgrid, 3) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>    Temp    RH #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  99.5   100 #> 2  99.8   100 #> 3 100     100

what: Examples of use of the functions
title: Humidity functions
text: Starting input data temperature relative humidity, wide range derived variables can computed. include absolute humidity, dew point temperature, mixing ratio, enthalpy, water vapor saturation pressure, actual water vapor pressure. Using relationship temperature, absolute humidity dew point can used adjust relative humidity. functions can used combination determine adjustments needed reach target relative humidity. approach assumes constant pressure, typical indoor situations. Adjustment Methods: Changing Absolute Humidity: Increasing absolute humidity constant temperature increase RH. Decreasing absolute humidity constant temperature decrease RH. Changing Temperature: Decreasing temperature constant absolute humidity increase RH. Increasing temperature constant absolute humidity decrease RH. Using Dew Point: difference air temperature dew point indicates close air saturation. Adjusting temperature closer dew point increases RH, moving away decreases RH.
code: # Functions applied on the mydata dataset mydata |>    mutate(     Abs = calcAH(Temp, RH),      DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH),      MR = calcMR(Temp, RH),      Enthalpy = calcEnthalpy(Temp, RH),      Pws = calcPws(Temp),      Pw = calcPw(Temp, RH),      Temp_calc = calcTemp(RH, DewPoint),     RH_AH_calc = calcRH(Temp, Abs),     RH_DP_calc = calcRH_DP(Temp, DewPoint)     ) |>   head() |>   glimpse() #> Rows: 6 #> Columns: 14 #> $ Site       <chr> "London", "London", "London", "London", "London", "London" #> $ Sensor     <chr> "Room 1", "Room 1", "Room 1", "Room 1", "Room 1", "Room 1" #> $ Date       <dttm> 2024-01-01 00:00:00, 2024-01-01 00:15:00, 2024-01-01 00:29:… #> $ Temp       <dbl> 21.8, 21.8, 21.8, 21.7, 21.7, 21.7 #> $ RH         <dbl> 36.8, 36.7, 36.6, 36.6, 36.5, 36.2 #> $ Abs        <dbl> 7.052415, 7.033251, 7.014087, 6.973723, 6.954670, 6.897508 #> $ DewPoint   <dbl> 6.383970, 6.344456, 6.304848, 6.216205, 6.176529, 6.056922 #> $ MR         <dbl> 5.959586, 5.943237, 5.926888, 5.890432, 5.874186, 5.825453 #> $ Enthalpy   <dbl> 37.16251, 37.12097, 37.07942, 36.88466, 36.84338, 36.71955 #> $ Pws        <dbl> 26.13122, 26.13122, 26.13122, 25.97199, 25.97199, 25.97199 #> $ Pw         <dbl> 9.616288, 9.590156, 9.564025, 9.505748, 9.479776, 9.401860 #> $ Temp_calc  <dbl> 21.8, 21.8, 21.8, 21.7, 21.7, 21.7 #> $ RH_AH_calc <dbl> 36.8, 36.7, 36.6, 36.6, 36.5, 36.2 #> $ RH_DP_calc <dbl> 36.8, 36.7, 36.6, 36.6, 36.5, 36.2 # Changing Absolute Humidity  mydata |>    mutate(     # Absolute humidity, AH     AH = calcAH(Temp, RH),      # Dew point, DP         DP = calcDP(Temp, RH),          # Target RH         tRH = 50,                    # AH*: AHstar new absolute humidity for target relative humidity, using tRH         AHstar = calcAH(Temp, tRH),              # Difference in measured AH and AH*          dAH = AHstar - AH,          # Check         AHcheck = AHstar - (AH + dAH),          # RH*: RH star - the new RH using AH* and measured temperature         RHstarAH = calcRH(Temp, AHstar),          # Difference from target         RHerrorAH = RHstarAH - tRH,          ) |>   ggplot() +   geom_line(aes(Date, Temp), col = "red", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line(aes(Date, RH), col = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +    geom_line(aes(Date, AH), col = "green", alpha = 0.5) +    geom_line(aes(Date, RHstarAH), col = "darkblue") +   geom_line(aes(Date, AHstar), col = "darkgreen") +    geom_line(aes(Date, RHerrorAH)) +    labs(title = "Using absolute humidity to adjust relative humidity",         subtitle = "Measure absolute humidity (light green), new absolute humidity (dark green),        resultant relative humidity (dark blue)") +   theme_bw() # Changing Temperature mydata |>    mutate(     # Absolute humidity, AH     AH = calcAH(Temp, RH),      # Dew point, DP         DP = calcDP(Temp, RH),          # Target RH         tRH = 50,                    # T*: Tstar new temperature at target RH using dew point         Tstar = calcTemp(tRH, DP),          # Difference in measure temperature and new temperature         dTemp = Tstar - Temp,          # Check         Tcheck = Tstar - (Temp + dTemp),          # RHstarT: New humidity with new temperature          RHstarT = calcRH_DP(Tstar, DP),         # Error          RHerrorT = RHstarT - tRH         ) |>   ggplot() +   geom_line(aes(Date, Temp), col = "red", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line(aes(Date, RH), col = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +    geom_line(aes(Date, Tstar), col = "darkred") +   geom_line(aes(Date, RHstarT), col = "darkblue") +   geom_line(aes(Date, RHerrorT)) +    labs(title = "Using temperature to adjust relative humidity") +   theme_bw() # Using Dew Point mydata |>   mutate(     # Absolute humidity, AH     AH = calcAH(Temp, RH),     # Dew point, DP         DP = calcDP(Temp, RH),         # Target RH         tRH = 50,         # DP*: DPstarAH new dew point at target RH using absolute humidity         DPstarAH = calcDP(Temp, tRH),         # RH*: New relative humidity at new dew point         RHstarDP = calcRH_DP(Temp, DPstarAH),         # Error          RHerrorDP = RHstarDP - tRH         ) |>   ggplot() +   geom_line(aes(Date, Temp), col = "red", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line(aes(Date, RH), col = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line(aes(Date, DP), col = "hotpink") +   geom_line(aes(Date, DPstarAH), col = "purple") +   geom_line(aes(Date, RHstarDP), col = "darkblue") +   geom_line(aes(Date, RHerrorDP)) +   labs(title = "Using dew point to adjust relative humidity") +   theme_bw()

title: Humidity functions
text: calcTemp temperature calculations explored determine accuracy temperature derivation using dew point relative humidity. Visualisation temperature calculation original temperature relative humidity. temperature calculation presented filled contour plot calculated temperature using calcTemp dew point calculated using calcDP.  Visualisation temperature calculation original temperature relative humidity. plot aims validate accuracy consistency temperature calculations based relative humidity dew point.
code: head(mydata) |> dplyr::mutate(DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH), Temp2 = calcTemp(RH, DewPoint)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH DewPoint Temp2 #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8     6.38  21.8 #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7     6.34  21.8 #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6     6.30  21.8 #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6     6.22  21.7 #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5     6.18  21.7 #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2     6.06  21.7  TRHgrid |>   mutate(     DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH),     TempCalc = calcTemp(RH, DewPoint)     ) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = TempCalc)) +    geom_contour_filled(bins = 20, breaks = seq(-50, 100, 10)) +   labs(title = "Calculated temperature vs actual",         subtitle = "calcDP(Temp, RH) > DewPoint > TempCalc") +    theme_minimal() TRHgrid |>   mutate(     DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH),     TempCalc = calcTemp(RH, DewPoint),      Temp_error = TempCalc - Temp     ) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = Temp_error)) +    geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +    labs(title = "Difference between calculated temperature vs actual",         subtitle = "calcDP(Temp, RH) > DewPoint > TempCalc - Temp (original)") +    theme_minimal()

title: Humidity functions
text: calcRH calcRH_DP section investigates relative humidity calculations using derivations dew point absolute humidity. Contour plots demonstrate relationships calculated original relative humidity values errors.     Using absolute humidity generates errors low temperatures high relative humidities may become significant exponentiate used machine learning applications. recommended wary using calcAH function low temperature high relative humidity.
code: TRHgrid |>   mutate(     DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH),     RH_DPcalc = calcRH_DP(Temp, DewPoint)     ) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = RH_DPcalc)) +    geom_contour_filled(bins = 20, breaks = seq(0, 100, 5)) +    labs(title = "Calculated humidity via dew point vs actual",         subtitle = "calcDP(Temp, RH) > DewPoint > RHcalc") +    theme_minimal() TRHgrid |>   mutate(     DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH),     RH_DPcalc = calcRH_DP(Temp, DewPoint),      RH_DP_error = RH_DPcalc - RH     ) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = RH_DP_error)) +    labs(title = "Calculated humidity vs actual",         subtitle = "calcDP(Temp, RH) > DewPoint > RHcalc - RH (original)") +   geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +    theme_minimal() TRHgrid |>   mutate(RH_AHcalc = calcRH(Temp, calcAH(Temp, RH))) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = RH_AHcalc)) +    geom_contour_filled(bins = 20, breaks = seq(0, 100, 5)) +    labs(title = "Calculated humidity via absolute humidity vs actual",         subtitle = "calcAH(Temp, RH) > Absolute humidity > RHcalc - RH (original)") +   theme_minimal() TRHgrid |>   mutate(RH_AH_error = calcRH(Temp, calcAH(Temp, RH)) - RH) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = RH_AH_error)) +    labs(title = "Calculated humidity via absolute humidity vs actual",         subtitle = "calcAH(Temp, RH) > Absolute humidity > RHcalc - RH (original)") +   geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +    theme_minimal()

text: calcMR
code: TRHgrid |>   mutate(MR = calcMR(Temp, RH)) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = MR)) +    geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +    theme_minimal() TRHgrid |>   mutate(MR_log10 = calcMR(Temp, RH) |> log10()) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = MR_log10)) +    geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +    theme_minimal()

title: Humidity functions
text: calcEnthalpy
code: TRHgrid |>   mutate(Enthalpy = calcEnthalpy(Temp, RH)) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = Enthalpy)) +    geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +    theme_minimal() TRHgrid |>   mutate(Enthalpy_log10 = calcEnthalpy(Temp, RH) |> log10()) |>   ggplot(aes(Temp, RH, z = Enthalpy_log10)) +    geom_contour_filled(bins = 20) +    theme_minimal()

title: Tools for Conservation Science
text: ConSciR R package specifically designed assist conservators scientists providing toolkit performing calculations streamlining common tasks conservation. ConSciR designed : Conservators working museums, galleries, heritage sites Conservation scientists researchers Cultural heritage professionals involved preventive conservation Students educators conservation heritage science programs Learn ConSciR : ConSciR webpage ConSciR Github page: ConSciR Github

title: Tools for Conservation Science
text: Calculations: dew point, absolute humidity, water vapour pressure, mixing ratio, enthalpy Graphs: TRH plots, psychometric chart Summaries: percentiles Analysis: Tidying data:

title: Tools for Conservation Science
text: can install development version ConSciR GitHub : --

title: Tools for Conservation Science
text: basic example commons tasks: Load packages Pre-loaded dataset availabe testing Use functions perform calculations, example use existing data add dew point absolute humidity. Performance metrics also added like lifetime multiplier, preservation index mould calculations. Combine analysis graphs  Use analysis functions, example estimate mould growth
code: library(ConSciR) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) # My TRH data head(mydata) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8 #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7 #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6 #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6 #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5 #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2 # Peform calculations head(mydata) |>   mutate(     DewP = calcDP(Temp, RH),      Abs = calcAH(Temp, RH),      LifeTime = calcLM(Temp, RH, EA = 100),      PI = calcPI(Temp, RH)     ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 9 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH  DewP   Abs LifeTime    PI #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8  6.38  7.05     1.85  45.3 #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7  6.34  7.03     1.85  45.4 #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6  6.30  7.01     1.85  45.5 #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6  6.22  6.97     1.85  46.1 #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5  6.18  6.95     1.86  46.2 #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2  6.06  6.90     1.86  46.6 mydata |>   mutate(DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH)) |>   graph_TRH() +    geom_line(aes(Date, DewPoint), col = "purple", size = 1) +    theme_minimal() mydata |>   calcMould(Temp = "Temp", RH = "RH") |>   ggplot() +   geom_area(aes(Date, mould), fill = "darkgreen", size = 1) +   labs(title = "Probabilty of mould", x = "", y = "Mould risk") +    theme_minimal()

title: Calculate dew point — calcDP
text: Function calculate dew point (°C) temperature (°C) relative humidity (%).

title: Calculate dew point — calcDP
text: https://bmcnoldy.earth.miami.edu/Humidity.html

title: Calculate dew point — calcDP
text: Temp Temperature (°Celsius) RH Relative Humidity (0-100%)

title: Calculate dew point — calcDP
text: Td, Dew Point (°Celsius)

title: Calculate dew point — calcDP
text: dew point calculated using following equation derived August-Roche-Magnus approximation: $$T_d\left(DewP\right)=\frac{234.04\times\log\left(\frac{RH}{100}\right)+\frac{17.625\times Temp}{243.04+Temp}}{17.625-\log\left(\frac{RH}{100}\right)-\frac{17.625\times Temp}{243.04+Temp}}$$ : T_d dew point temperature degrees Celsius. Temp air temperature degrees Celsius. RH relative humidity percent. Calculation valid : 0°C < T < 60°C 1% < RH < 100% 0°C < Td < 50°C

title: Calculate dew point — calcDP
text: Arden Buck equation also available source R code.

title: Calculate enthalpy — calcEnthalpy
text: Function calculate enthalpy temperature (°C) relative humidity (%). using dataframe, columns ideally named "Temp" "RH". Example: ambient temperature 20B0C relative humidity 50 Pw = Pws(20B0C) B7 50/100 = 11.69 hPa X = 621.9907 B7 11.69/(1013-11.69) = 7.26 g/kg h = 20 B7 (1.01 + 0.00189 B7 7.26) + 2.5 B7 7.26 = 38.62 kJ/kg

title: Calculate enthalpy — calcEnthalpy
text: Temp Temperature (°Celsius) RH Relative Humidity (0-100%)

title: Calculate enthalpy — calcEnthalpy
text: h Enthalpy (kJ/kg)

title: Calculate enthalpy — calcEnthalpy
code: calcEnthalpy(20, 50) #> [1] 38.6333  head(mydata) |> dplyr::mutate(Enthalpy = calcEnthalpy(Temp, RH)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH Enthalpy #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8     37.2 #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7     37.1 #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6     37.1 #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6     36.9 #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5     36.8 #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2     36.7

title: Calculate humidity ratio — calcHR
text: function uses following steps: 1. Calculate mixing ratio using calcMR function. 2. Convert mixing ratio humidity ratio using formula: HR = MR / (1 + MR) MR mixing ratio HR humidity ratio. Note: function requires calcMR function available environment.

Calculate mixing ratio — calcMR
text: Function calculate mixing ratio temperature (°C) relative humidity (%). using dataframe, columns ideally named "Temp" "RH". X Mixing ratio (mass water vapour / mass dry gas) Pw = Pws(40°C) = 73.75 hPa X = 621.9907 B7 73.75/(998-73.75) = 49.63 g/kg

title: Calculate Mould Growth Probability — calcMould
text: function calculates probability mould growth based temperature relative humidity data.

title: Calculate Mould Growth Probability — calcMould
text: mydata data frame containing temperature relative humidity data. Date name column data containing date data. Default "Date". Temp name column data containing temperature data. Default "Temp". RH name column data containing relative humidity data. Default "RH".

title: Calculate Mould Growth Probability — calcMould
text: data frame additional columns: time_diff Time difference consecutive measurements mould_prob Probability mould growth time step mould Cumulative mould growth probability

title: Calculate Mould Growth Probability — calcMould
code: head(mydata) |> calcMould(Date = "Date", Temp = "Temp", RH = "RH") #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH TempMould RHMould days_mould #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8        22      37         NA #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7        22      37         NA #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6        22      37         NA #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6        22      37         NA #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5        22      36         NA #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2        22      36         NA #> # ℹ 3 more variables: time_diff <drtn>, mould_prob <dbl>, mould <dbl>

Calculate Mould Growth Probability Using Various Models — calcMould_models

text: results different take care interpretation.

title: Calculate Mould Growth Probability Using Various Models — calcMould_models
code: calcMould_models(20, 80, "Sedlbauer") #> [1] 74.4 calcMould_models(25, 70, "VIR") #> [1] 79.125 calcMould_models(22, 85, "Isaksson") #> [1] 0.01162899   head(mydata) |>   dplyr::mutate(     RH_crit_Sedlbauer = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, "Sedlbauer"),     RH_crit_VIR = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, "VIR"),     Mould_daily_dose = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, "Isaksson")     ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 8 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH RH_crit_Sedlbauer RH_crit_VIR #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8              73.5        79.0 #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7              73.5        79.0 #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6              73.5        79.0 #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6              73.5        79.0 #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5              73.5        79.0 #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2              73.5        79.0 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: Mould_daily_dose <dbl>

title: Calculate Preservation Index — calcPI
text: Chemical degradation cellulose acetate – Preservation Index Image Permanence Institute (IPI) (Reilly et al., 1995) k = [RH%] × 5.9 × 1012 × e(-90300/(8.314 × T)) PI = 1/k

title: Calculate Preservation Index — calcPI
text: Tim Padfield, 2004

title: Calculate Preservation Index — calcPI
text: Temp Temperature (Celsius) RH Relative Humidity (0-100%)

title: Calculate Preservation Index — calcPI
text: PI Preservation Index, expected lifetime 1/k

title: Calculate Preservation Index — calcPI
text: Reilly et al., 1995 Padfield, T. 2004. Preservation Index Time Weighted Preservation Index. https://www.conservationphysics.org/twpi/twpi_01.html

what: Calculate relative humidity from temperature and absolute humidity — calcRH
title: Calculate relative humidity from temperature and absolute humidity — calcRH
text: Function calculate relative humidity (%) temperature (°C) absolute humidity (g/m^3)

title: Calculate relative humidity from temperature and absolute humidity — calcRH
Calculate relative humidity from temperature and absolute humidity — calcRH
text: Temp Temperature (°Celsius) Abs Absolute Humidity (g/m³)

title: Calculate relative humidity from temperature and absolute humidity — calcRH
text: Relative Humidity (0-100%). Error increases extreme temperature relative humidity.

title: Calculate relative humidity from temperature and absolute humidity — calcRH
text: relative humidity calculated using following equation: $$RH=\frac{1}{2165\times\frac{P_{vs}}{(Temp + 273.15)\timesAbs}}$$ $$RH=\frac{1}{2165}$$ : \(RH\) relative humidity (fraction) \(P_{vs}\) saturation vapor pressure (Pa) \(Temp\) temperature (°C) \(Abs\) absolute humidity (g/m^3)

title: Calculate relative humidity from temperature and absolute humidity — calcRH
code: # Calculate RH for temperature of 20°C and absolute humidity of 8.645471 g/m^3 calcRH(20, 8.645471) #> [1] 50.08653  calcRH(20, calcAH(20, 50)) #> [1] 49.99999

what: Calculate Relative Humidity from temperature and dew point — calcRH_DP
title: Calculate Relative Humidity from temperature and dew point — calcRH_DP
text: Function calculate relative humidity (%) temperature (°C) dew point (°C)

title: Calculate Relative Humidity from temperature and dew point — calcRH_DP
Calculate Relative Humidity from temperature and dew point — calcRH_DP
text: https://bmcnoldy.earth.miami.edu/Humidity.html

title: Calculate Relative Humidity from temperature and dew point — calcRH_DP
text: Temp Temperature (°Celsius) DewP Td, Dew Point (°Celsius)

title: Calculate Relative Humidity from temperature and dew point — calcRH_DP
text: Relative Humidity (0-100%)

title: Calculate Relative Humidity from temperature and dew point — calcRH_DP
text: relative humidity calculated using following equation derived August-Roche-Magnus approximation: $$RH=100\times\frac{\exp\left(\frac{\left(17.625\times DewP\right)}{\left(243.04+DewP\right)}\right)}{\exp\left(\frac{\left(17.625\times Temp\right)}{\left(243.04+Temp\right)}\right)}$$ : RH relative humidity percent Temp air temperature °C DewP dew point temperature °C

title: Calculate Relative Humidity from temperature and dew point — calcRH_DP
code: # RH at air tempertaure of 20C and dew point of 15C calcRH_DP(20, 15) #> [1] 72.93877  calcRH_DP(20, calcDP(20, 50)) #> [1] 50  head(mydata) |> dplyr::mutate(DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH), RH2 = calcRH_DP(Temp, DewPoint)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH DewPoint   RH2 #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8     6.38  36.8 #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7     6.34  36.7 #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6     6.30  36.6 #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6     6.22  36.6 #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5     6.18  36.5 #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2     6.06  36.2

what: Calculate Temperature from relative humidity and dew point — calcTemp
title: Calculate Temperature from relative humidity and dew point — calcTemp
text: function calculates temperature (°C) relative humidity (%) dew point temperature (°C).

title: Calculate Temperature from relative humidity and dew point — calcTemp
Calculate Temperature from relative humidity and dew point — calcTemp
text: RH Relative Humidity (0-100%) DewP Td, Dew Point (Celsius)

title: Calculate Temperature from relative humidity and dew point — calcTemp
text: Temp, Temperature (°Celsius)

title: Calculate Temperature from relative humidity and dew point — calcTemp
text: temperature calculated using following equation derived August-Roche-Magnus approximation: $$TempC=\frac{243.04\times\left(\frac{17.625\times DewP}{243.04+DewP}-\log\left(\frac{RH}{100}\right)\right)}{17.625+\log\left(\frac{RH}{100}\right)-\frac{17.625\times DewP}{243.04+DewP}}$$ : Temp calculated temperature Celsius RH relative humidity percent DewP dew point temperature Celsius

title: Calculate Temperature from relative humidity and dew point — calcTemp
code: # Calculate temperature for RH of 50% and dew point of 15°C calcTemp(50, 15) #> [1] 26.24387  calcTemp(50, calcDP(20, 50)) #> [1] 20  head(mydata) |> dplyr::mutate(DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH), Temp2 = calcTemp(RH, DewPoint)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH DewPoint Temp2 #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8     6.38  21.8 #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7     6.34  21.8 #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6     6.30  21.8 #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6     6.22  21.7 #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5     6.18  21.7 #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2     6.06  21.7
